title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dizziness

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with dizziness because of its unique combination of active compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids. These natural ingredients work together to strengthen blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is responsible for balance and equilibrium. By addressing these underlying issues, maidenhair tree tincture can help alleviate dizziness and vertigo, providing a sense of stability and calmness. This traditional remedy has been used for centuries to treat various forms of dizziness and motion sickness, offering a natural and effective solution for those struggling with these debilitating symptoms.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Maidenhair Tree tincture helps to balance and stabilize the nervous system, reducing symptoms of dizziness by adapting to changes in the body's physiological state.

Cephalic and Cardiac Tonic Action: The tincture has a tonifying effect on the brain, heart, and blood vessels, which can help to regulate blood pressure and circulation, alleviating dizziness caused by hypotension or other cardiovascular issues.

Anti-Anxiety and Sedative Effects: Maidenhair Tree contains flavonoids that have been shown to possess anti-anxiogenic and sedative properties, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety and unease that can contribute to dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree are rich in bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and saponins that help to stabilize blood pressure, reducing episodes of dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning after dew has dried ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Chop 20-30 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 70% proof vodka in a glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks agitating the mixture twice daily allowing for optimal extraction time.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and labeling accordingly for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with dizziness because it has a natural sedative effect on the nervous system, calming the symptoms of vertigo and dizziness. The active compounds in kava, such as kavalactones, interact with neurotransmitters to reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate feelings of dizziness. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, kava tinctures help alleviate dizziness caused by various conditions, including motion sickness, labyrinthitis, and benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV).
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Kava's anxiolytic effects can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety-related dizziness by decreasing GABA receptor binding.
Muscle relaxant properties: Kava's muscle-relaxing compounds, such as kavalactones, can help ease muscle tension and spasms that may contribute to dizziness.

Anticonvulsant properties: Some kavalactones in kava tinctures have been shown to have anticonvulsant effects, which can help stabilize the nervous system and prevent dizziness caused by seizures or other neurological disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and lactones, the roots are a rich source of kavalactones, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
Rhyzomes: As the underground stems of the kava plant, rhyzomes contain high levels of kavalactones, making them an effective ingredient in tinctures for treating dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh kava root and wash it thoroughly with pure water.
Cut the kava root into small pieces and soak them in 500 milliliters of vodka for 6 weeks.
Shake the mixture daily and check on its color which should be light brown and slightly thickened.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2 months before using it to alleviate dizziness symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with dizziness because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system. The flavonoids present in maypop, a type of passionflower, have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic properties, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often contribute to dizziness. Additionally, maypop's sedative effects can help alleviate symptoms of vertigo by soothing the inner ear and promoting balance. As a result, maypop tinctures can provide effective relief from episodes of dizziness, allowing individuals to regain their equilibrium and feel more grounded.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Maypop tincture's anxiolytic properties help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress, which are common underlying causes of dizziness, by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.

Vasoconstrictive: The vasoconstrictive effects of maypop tincture can help to alleviate dizziness caused by vasovagal syncope (the sudden drop in blood pressure) by constricting blood vessels and increasing blood pressure.
Anti-inflammatory: Maypop tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the inner ear, which is a common cause of vertigo and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in calcium and magnesium, which can help stabilize blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce dizziness caused by inflammation or irritation in the inner ear.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh maypop flowers and leaves at dawn or just before sunset for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested material using a dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Combine one part dried maypop material with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tinctures helps with dizziness because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. This can help to stabilize blood pressure and alleviate feelings of lightheadedness and vertigo. The herb's natural sedative properties also work to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for dizziness. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the brain, skullcap tinctures provide a soothing remedy for individuals experiencing frequent or debilitating episodes of dizziness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic effects: Skullcap's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm muscle spasms in the inner ear, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness and vertigo.
Anxiolytic effects: Skullcap's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties help to alleviate stress and anxiety-related dizziness by promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of panic or unease.
Vasodilatory effects: Skullcap's vasodilatory properties help to widen blood vessels, which can improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, reducing dizziness caused by inadequate blood supply.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, skullcap roots are used to create a calming and soothing tincture that helps alleviate dizziness.
Rhyzomes: Containing a unique blend of chemical constituents, skullcap rhyzomes are prized for their ability to stabilize blood pressure and reduce dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest scutellaria lateriflora flowers and leaves in mid-summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested material using shade or low-temperature dehydration methods within two days.
Combine one part dried skullcap with five parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with dizziness because it has a profound effect on the nervous system. The herb's active compounds, such as valerenic acid, interact with neurotransmitters to calm and soothe the mind and body. This relaxation response can help alleviate feelings of lightheadedness and dizziness by reducing anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for these symptoms. Additionally, valerian tinctures have been shown to improve blood flow and circulation, further helping to combat dizziness and maintain a sense of balance and stability.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative properties help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of lightheadedness and dizziness by slowing down heart rate and blood pressure.

Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dizziness and lightheadedness by reducing inflammation and improving circulation.

GABA-mimetic properties: Valerian has been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, helping to increase GABA levels and promote a sense of calm and relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have sedative and anti-anxiety properties, making it effective in treating dizziness.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are rich in essential oils that help to calm the nervous system, reducing symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots or purchase dried roots.
Dry the harvested or purchased roots using air circulation and shade.
Cut the dried roots into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder.
Mix one part powdered valerian root with three parts high-proof vodka.
Allow the tincture to steep for six weeks, shaking daily, before straining and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cat's claw tinctures helps with dizziness because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including glycosides, alkaloids, and flavonoids. These compounds have been shown to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and increase blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness. Additionally, cat's claw has been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health, which is often closely linked with balance and equilibrium. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Cat's claw tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dizziness and vertigo by reducing inflammation in the inner ear and promoting healthy blood flow.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in cat's claw tincture can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the inner ear, which is often associated with dizziness and vertigo, and promote a sense of balance and stability.

Vasodilatory effects: Cat's claw tincture has been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help improve blood flow to the brain and inner ear, reducing the symptoms of dizziness and vertigo by increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery to these areas.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality uncaria tomentosa roots or stems in dry weather conditions.
Wash the harvested plant material thoroughly with clean water and gently pat it dry.
Combine 10 grams of dried uncaria tomentosa with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a dark glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for eight weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and store it in amber glass bottles, using one to two milliliters up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine tinctures helps with dizziness because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds that work together to alleviate symptoms of vertigo. The tincture's active ingredients, including flavonoids and alkaloids, have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, reducing feelings of lightheadedness and disorientation. Additionally, yellow jessamine has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to stabilize equilibrium and reduce the occurrence of dizziness caused by anxiety or stress.

remedy_recipe: Harvest gelsemium sempervirens roots and stems, cutting them into small pieces, ensuring freshness and potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade or air drying methods for 7-10 days, monitoring moisture levels carefully.
Grind the dried plant material into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder, taking care to avoid overheating.
Combine 1 part powdered gelsemium sempervirens with 2 parts high-proof vodka or everclear, transferring the mixture to glass jars for maceration.
Store the jarred mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily, before straining and filtering the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterfly milkweed tinctures helps with dizziness because they contain a unique blend of herbal compounds that work together to stabilize the body's vital energies. The tincture's active ingredients, such as rotenone and cardenolides, have been shown to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of vertigo and lightheadedness. remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: These compounds help regulate heart rhythm and improve blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate dizziness caused by circulatory issues.

Amino acids: Milkweed tincture contains various amino acids, including tryptophan and tyrosine, which are involved in serotonin and dopamine production, respectively. These neurotransmitters can help regulate blood pressure and alleviate feelings of dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the butterfly milkweed plant contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
Seeds: Butterfly milkweed seeds are rich in fatty acids and other nutrients that can help stabilize blood pressure and reduce feelings of lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh asclepias tuberosa roots and leaves, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Chop the dried asclepias tuberosa into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine one part chopped asclepias tuberosa with three parts high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete submersion.
Store the tincture mixture in a cool, dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate solubilization.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with dizziness because of its natural properties that can help calm the stomach and ease nausea. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate feelings of dizziness and vertigo. Additionally, ginger's natural anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce blood vessel constriction, which can contribute to lightheadedness. By promoting a healthy gut and reducing inflammation, herbal ginger tinctures can provide relief from dizziness and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal ginger tinctures, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dizziness and lightheadedness.

Digestive-soothing properties: Ginger's ability to soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea and vomiting can help alleviate symptoms of inner ear inflammation and other conditions that can cause dizziness, such as vestibular disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate dizziness symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of ginger are rich in sesquiterpenes, which can help to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea associated with dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots or purchase high-quality dried ginger powder.
Chop the ginger into small pieces and soak them in vodka or high-proof grain alcohol for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding the solids after 2 hours.
Mix the extracted tincture with distilled water to create a 1:5 dilution ratio and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.
Take 20-30 drops of the ginger tincture diluted with water three times daily as needed for dizziness relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with dizziness because of its calming and soothing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a healthy plant on a sunny day at dawn.

Chop the rinsed peppermint leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Soak the chopped peppermint leaves in 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part herbs to 4 parts liquid for four weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and transfer it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with dizziness because of its soothing properties that calm the nervous system. Combine one part dried chamomilla flowers with one part 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar carefully.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily meticulously.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle after preparation is complete.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa leaves and flowers, ensuring they are clean and free of contaminants.

Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking the container daily to facilitate extraction of the plant's active compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with dizziness because its active compound, casticin, has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of lightheadedness and vertigo. Store the mixture at room temperature for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.

Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the filtered tincture in a cool dark place and use within 2 years taking 20-30 drops 1-2 times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with dizziness because it contains flavonoids and sesquiterpenes that have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of lightheadedness and disorientation. Additionally, pot marigold's anti-anxiety properties can help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of panic and stress that can exacerbate dizziness. By addressing these underlying issues, pot marigold tinctures can provide natural relief from dizzy spells.
remedy_properties: Calming effect on the nervous system: Pot marigold's sedative and anxiolytic properties help to calm the nerves, reducing feelings of dizziness and vertigo caused by anxiety or stress.

Antihistamine and anti-inflammatory action: The tincture's antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms of Meniere's disease, a condition characterized by dizziness, vertigo, and hearing loss.

Stabilizing effect on blood vessels: Pot marigold's flavonoids and other compounds may help to stabilize blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the likelihood of dizziness caused by decreased blood flow to the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are valued for their ability to calm digestive issues and reduce symptoms of dizziness.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, seeds are often used to soothe and stabilize the nervous system, alleviating feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and essential oils.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by rinsing them gently in cold running water removing any debris.
Combine 100 grams of clean calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for six weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure consistent extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dizziness are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, used to alleviate symptoms of vertigo, lightheadedness, and balance issues. These natural remedies help by stabilizing blood pressure, calming the nervous system, and improving circulation, which can reduce the sensation of dizziness. Examples include ginger, peppermint, and lavender tinctures, which can be taken sublingually or added to tea. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals with chronic dizziness may experience improved balance, reduced anxiety, and increased confidence, allowing them to participate in daily activities with greater ease and independence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dizziness with herbal tinctures is to combine specific formulas tailored to address underlying causes. Ginkgo biloba and passionflower tinctures help alleviate vertigo and balance issues by improving blood flow and reducing anxiety. Valerian root tincture, known for its calming properties, can also provide relief from dizziness caused by stress or nervousness. Blend these herbs in a balanced ratio to create an effective treatment plan.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dizziness that are treated with herbal tinctures are nausea, vertigo, and lightheadedness. These conditions often share similar symptoms such as a feeling of unsteadiness or imbalance. Herbal tinctures like ginger, peppermint, and lemon balm are commonly used to treat these ailments by calming the stomach and soothing the nervous system. They can help alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
